item.title: Tata’s UK Plant Wins Exemption From US Steel Tariff Rules
item.description: Tata Steel UK has secured a US tariff exemption that will allow it to export steel processed in Britain under the lower rate applied to UK shipments, even though the raw material was made overseas.

item.title: German Gas Storage to Fill Up Without Aid, Market Manager Says
item.description: Germany’s gas market manager reiterated that it will not intervene on behalf of the government to refill the country’s vast storage facilities, saying that levels should start rising soon.

item.title: Niger Boosts Stake in China-Backed Pipeline as Beijing Seeks to Secure Supply
item.description: Niger secured a stake in a China-backed oil pipeline after renegotiating export terms, as Beijing steps up efforts to secure reliable crude supplies amid global market disruptions.

item.title: College Kids Don’t Want Your AI
item.description: The unique threat artificial intelligence poses to students’ education and their hopes of someday landing a job has made the university a powerful crucible for anti-AI resistance.

item.title: La Caisse Arm Buys Database Firm ISC in $872 Million Deal
item.description: The infrastructure arm of Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec will take Canadian database company Information Services Corp. private in a deal worth C$1.2 billion ($872 million).

item.title: Polish Lawmakers Want Central Bank Governor to Testify in Probe
item.description: Polish central bank Governor Adam Glapinski will be called to testify before a parliamentary committee following renewed conflict with the ruling coalition.

item.title: Shell Wins Investor Backing for Higher Executive Bonuses
item.description: Shell Plc shareholders approved a new policy that could award larger bonuses to executive directors, moving the company closer to compensation levels at its US rivals.

item.title: Distressed-Debt Deals Often End in ‘Hard Default,’ Moody’s Warns
item.description: About a quarter of companies that go through a distressed exchange event end up in so-called “hard defaults” such as bankruptcy or a missed payment, according to a Moody’s Analytics report.

item.title: US Pending Home Sales Increase for a Third Straight Month
item.description: Pending sales of previously owned US homes rose for a third straight month in April, indicating firmer underlying demand at the start of the spring selling season.

item.title: Bessent Tells Allies ‘No Room for Excuses’ on Iran Sanctions
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent exhorted US allies to join the US in “aggressively” enforcing sanctions on Iran and other malicious actors and said there was “no room for excuses” for those who wish to crack down on terror financing.

item.title: High Speed 2 to Cost up to £103 Billion After More Delays
item.description: The UK’s High Speed 2 rail line will cost between £87.7 billion ($117 billion) and £102.7 billion and open by 2036 at the earliest, Transport Secretary Heidi Alexander said, revealing the latest budget increase and delay to the troubled project.

item.title: French Grid Keeps Nuclear Reactors Online Despite Solar Surge
item.description: France’s grid operator is keeping extra nuclear reactors online to keep the system stable despite strong solar power generation, contributing to a supply surplus and deeply discounted electricity prices.

item.title: US 30-Year Yield Hits Highest Since 2007 on Inflation Angst
item.description: Yields on the US Treasury’s longest-dated bond rose to the highest level in almost two decades as investor concern over accelerating inflation fuels a selloff across global debt markets.

item.title: Investor Angst Emerges in Riskiest Stocks Even as AI Trade Flies
item.description: Investors are starting to hedge the risk of a drop in the Russell 2000 Index of smaller companies, despite investors remaining wildly bullish for large-cap stocks.

item.title: Jeff Currie Says AI Boom Sets Up Commodities for Decade-Long Run
item.description: Veteran strategist Jeff Currie said the world is in the early stages of a commodity supercycle that may last another decade or more as the artificial intelligence buildout collides with chronic underinvestment in energy and materials capacity.

item.title: Sinomine Seeks $760 Million for African Lithium, Copper Projects
item.description: China’s Sinomine Resource Group Co. is seeking as much as 5.2 billion yuan ($764 million) to fund projects in Africa, as it steps up investments in raw materials critical to the energy transition.

item.title: Reform UK Threatens Legal Challenges to National Grid Projects
item.description: Reform UK threatened to use the nation’s courts to delay major electricity grid projects, opening a new political front against Britain’s plans to rapidly expand offshore wind power and modernize the energy system.

item.title: Ecolab Looks to Raise About $4 Billion to Fund CoolIT Takeover
item.description: Ecolab Inc. is seeking to sell around $4 billion of investment-grade bonds to help finance its acquisition of CoolIT Systems Inc., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Former FTX Executive Launches a ‘No Loss’ AI Trading Platform
item.description: At the European arm of failed crypto exchange FTX, Patrick Gruhn watched as traders repeatedly lost money on risky bets. Now, he’s launching a product where the savviest investors can turn a profit — even while putting zero capital at stake.

item.title: Scorching Summer Pushes India’s Power Demand to Record Again
item.description: India’s peak electricity demand climbed to a record for the second consecutive day as blistering heat pushed up use of cooling appliances such as air-conditioners.

item.title: Wall Street Banks Kick Off Loan Sale Tied to Warner Bros. Deal
item.description: Wall Street banks kicked off a loan sale Tuesday for Warner Bros Discovery Inc., with proceeds intended to refinance a temporary credit facility.

item.title: UK On Verge of Trade Deal With Gulf States, Says Head of GCC
item.description: The UK is set to finalize a long-awaited trade deal with the Gulf Cooperation Council on Wednesday, according to Saudi Arabian state media, deepening its ties with the oil-rich nations at a time of rising geopolitical tension and economic pressures.

item.title: Nagel Says ECB May ‘Have to Do Something’ as Iran Shock Persists
item.description: The European Central Bank may have to respond to the economic challenges arising from the conflict in the Middle East, according to Governing Council member Joachim Nagel.

item.title: Sherritt Halts Plan to Unwind Cuba Ventures Amid Sanctions Talks
item.description: Sherritt International Corp. reversed course on plans to unwind its Cuban business after further consultations with advisers and government officials, as the nickel producer scrambles to respond to tougher US sanctions on the island.

item.title: Canada Inflation Rises to 2.8% While Core Measures Ease
item.description: Higher energy prices drove up Canada’s inflation rate to 2.8% in April, reaching its highest level in nearly two years but coming in lower than economists had expected as core measures cooled.

item.title: NYSE’s Owner Plans Its Own Futures Market for Computing Power
item.description: Intercontinental Exchange Inc., owner of the New York Stock Exchange, is adding futures contracts for computing power as the market for tracking the price of what’s driving AI technology continues to develop.

item.title: G-7 Urges Fiscal Restraint in Face of War Shock to Economies
item.description: Group of Seven finance chiefs pledged not to overdo any fiscal aid as the Iran war raises growth and inflation risks to the global economy.

item.title: Why the Iran War Is a Boost for Stalled Russia-China Gas Pipeline
item.description: The Iran war and resulting energy crisis could inject new urgency into plans for a major natural gas pipeline between Russia and China.

item.title: Senegal Arrears to Arab Development Bank Highlight Debt Woes
item.description: The Arab Bank for Economic Development in Africa told investors last week that Senegal is in arrears with the lender, people familiar with the matter said, adding to signs of mounting financial strain on the government. The nation’s dollar bonds fell.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 19, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.4% as of 7:42 a.m. in New York as investors weigh the balances of market positioning, profit taking, rotation and dip buying in the absence of significant macro events since the close on Monday.
